tagline: Zdobądź coś skądś. Przywieź coś dla kogoś.

hero.body: Ktoś zawsze gdzieś jedzie. Zaoferuj wolne miejsce w bagażu, podziel się tym, co przywiozłeś, albo po prostu napisz, czego szukasz — właściwi ludzie spotykają się na jednej mapie. Dogadujecie się, przekazujecie, układ zostaje między wami.

home.editorialTitle: Jedna mapa. Trzy sposoby, by się spotkać.
home.editorialBody: Pojemność na wyjazdach, lokalne ogłoszenia i otwarte poszukiwania trafiają na tę samą powierzchnię odkrywania. Sąsiad może chcieć to, co masz w torbie; ktoś na twojej trasie może mieć miejsce na to, czego brakuje. My tylko łączymy—układ zostaje między wami, dopóki nie pojawią się płatności w aplikacji.
home.pillar1Body: Przeglądasz według miejsca i korytarza—gdzie jesteś, dokąd jedziesz—a nie tylko według flagi na mapie.
home.pillar2Body: Opublikuj wolne miejsce w bagażu na podróże, które i tak robisz, albo dodaj poszukiwanie i pozwól, by oferty same przyszły. Codzienne udostępnienia mają małą opłatę znalazcy; sloty na wyjazdach—uzgodnioną stawkę za slot. Dziś ceny stałe; później precyzyjniejsze rozliczenia, gdy uda się je wdrożyć porządnie.
home.pillar3Body: Ustalacie w czacie i płacicie osobiście lub poza aplikacją, dopóki nie pojawią się regulowane płatności in-app. Dziś nie dotykamy waszych pieniędzy.
home.ctaBandBody: Podziel się tym, co przywiozłeś, napisz, czego potrzebujesz, albo ogłoś miejsce na wyjeździe, gdy narzędzia wystartują—zawsze blisko, by spotkania były realne.

kpi.stripFootnote: Te liczby opisują kuratorski katalog, a nie GMV ani rejestracje na produkcji.

signInForm.linkOnWay: Magiczny link jest w drodze na {email}.
signInForm.localDevHint: Lokalnie: bez Resend link trafia do logów API (widoczny w
signInForm.errorTooMany: Za dużo prób. Spróbuj ponownie za minutę.
signInForm.errorServer: Usługa logowania jest niedostępna. Spróbuj ponownie.
signInForm.errorSend: Nie udało się wysłać linku. Sprawdź adres i spróbuj ponownie.

profilePanel.approxCityHint: Zapisujemy jako przybliżony obszar — nigdy dokładny adres.
profilePanel.preferredLanguageHint: Do e-maili i domyślnego języka interfejsu.
profilePanel.inboxBody: Dopasowania, podróże i wiadomości w jednym miejscu.
profilePanel.blockedBody: Zablokowane osoby nie mogą do Ciebie pisać i Ty do nich.
profilePanel.gdprBody: Możesz wyeksportować wszystkie dane lub usunąć konto w dowolnym momencie.

discover.metaDescription: Ogłoszenia w promieniu 25 km od Ciebie. Dokładne adresy pozostają prywatne — pinezki są lekko przesunięte dla bezpieczeństwa.
discover.heading: W promieniu 25 km od Ciebie.
discover.intro: Pinezki są lekko przesunięte, aby chronić dokładne adresy — nigdy ich nie pokazujemy. Dotknij karty, aby napisać.

about.metaDescription: Smak domu bez tajemniczych DM. Otwarta warstwa dla podróży diaspory, lokalnych podziałów i otwartych próśb.
about.title: Smak domu bez tajemniczych DM.
about.subtitle: Ludzie ciągle podróżują i przywożą sobie nawzajem rzeczy. Eushop to otwarta warstwa na to — zaczynamy od gęstych europejskich korytarzy.
about.p1: Eushop jest dla małych zachcianek między wizytami w domu: czekolady, która nigdzie nie smakuje tak samo, sera z lodówki babci, kiełbasy, która dla was znaczy „niedziela”.
about.p2: Nie jesteśmy importerem, magazynem ani kurierem. Jesteśmy od przedstawień: miejsce w walizce na prawdziwych trasach, otwarte prośby, lokalne dzielenie się między sąsiadami — widzialna tożsamość i czat aż do jasnego przekazania. Regulowane płatności w aplikacji tam, gdzie prawo pozwala; do tego czasu rozliczenie zostaje między wami.
about.p3: Jesteśmy mali z premedytacją. Dziś bez komercyjnych sprzedawców. Spotykajcie się publicznie, zachowujcie rozsądek i sąsiedzkie porcje.

trips.metaDescription: Zarezerwuj miejsce w wolnym bagażu osoby, która jedzie. Wyjazdy między miastami, co tydzień.
trips.heading: Nadchodzące wyjazdy.
trips.intro: Ktoś, kto jedzie w przyszłym tygodniu, publikuje wolne miejsca w bagażu. Zarezerwuj jedno na słoik Krówek, krąg Manchego, butelkę Mastiha. Pobieramy niewielką opłatę platformy od każdej potwierdzonej rezerwacji; cała reszta zostaje między wami a osobą podróżującą.
trips.emptyDescription: Eushop jest świeży i tablica wyjazdów jest cicha. Bądź pierwszą osobą, która udostępni wolne miejsca — albo dodaj poszukiwanie, a damy znać, gdy pojawi się pasująca trasa.
trips.errorDescription: We couldn’t reach the trips service. Try again in a moment, or browse open requests in the meantime.
trips.listSubtitle: People sharing spare slots in their next trip home.

tripDetail.outageDescription: We couldn’t load this trip. Try again shortly.
tripDetail.platformNote: Eushop adds a small platform fee on confirmation; everything else is yours.
tripDetail.noNotes: The traveller didn’t leave any notes.
tripDetail.reserveFullBody: Post a request and we’ll notify you when this traveller — or the next one on the route — opens a new trip.
tripDetail.sellerReservationsEmpty: No reservations yet. Buyers will appear here when they book.
tripDetail.buyerReservationsEmpty: You have no reservations on this trip yet.
tripDetail.activeReservationCount: {count, plural, one {# active reservation} other {# active reservations}}

tripForm.heading: Post a trip you’re already taking.
tripForm.intro: Tell us where you’re flying, when, and how much spare capacity you have. Buyers reserve slots from a single map; you keep all messaging in-app.
tripForm.slotsHint: How many separate parcels can you carry?
tripForm.feeHint: Buyers may offer more; the floor is what you set here.
tripForm.notesPlaceholder: Anything sellers should know — fragile only, no liquids, dropping at GDN airport, etc.

reservations.heading: Twoje rezerwacje
reservations.intro: Slots you’ve reserved on other people’s trips. Cancel before departure if your plans change; mark complete once the goods are in your hands.
reservations.emptyDescription: Browse open trips and reserve a slot to see it here.
reservations.errorDescription: The reservations service is unreachable. Try again in a moment.
reservations.signInTitle: Sign in to see your reservations.
reservations.paymentsNotice: Przy płatności online za miejsce w podróży może obowiązywać blokada na karcie do czasu potwierdzenia przez podróżnika. Zwroty, spory i chargebacki wynikają z Regulaminu i banku wydającego kartę.

reservationForm.heading: Reserve this slot
reservationForm.freeformPlaceholder: 1 kg of Wedel chocolate, 2 jars of ajvar, a copy of Eska Magazine…
reservationForm.freeformHint: Be specific — exact brand, count, and any acceptable substitutes.
reservationForm.feeHint: Minimum na ten przejazd: €{floor}. Wyższe oferty szybciej docierają.
reservationForm.confirmBody: You’re asking the traveller to bring {what} for €{fee}. They’ll confirm or decline within 24 hours.
reservationForm.successBody: We pinged the traveller. You’ll see updates on this page and in your inbox.
reservationForm.errorGeneric: Could not reserve right now. Please try again.
reservationForm.tripPaymentsFinePrint: Przy włączonym checkout możesz upoważnić blokadę na uzgodnioną opłatę za slot plus opłatę platformy Eushop (limit 1,50 € lub 12% opłaty za slot — mniejsza wartość). Pobranie następuje dopiero po potwierdzeniu przez podróżnika.

reservationPayment.holdExplainer: Blokujemy kwotę opłaty za slot i niewielką opłatę platformy Eushop. Pobranie następuje dopiero po potwierdzeniu przez podróżnika.
reservationPayment.disputesNote: Chargebacki i zwroty według reguł sieci kart; zobacz regulamin.
reservationPayment.stripeKeyMissing: Brak klucza publikowalnego Stripe — rezerwacja zapisana; sprzedawca potwierdzi bez blokady online, dopóki nie ustawisz NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.

reservationActions.confirmCancel: Cancel this reservation? The traveller will be notified.
reservationActions.confirmComplete: Mark this reservation as complete? This releases payment to the traveller.

tripDetailMobile.pickItemBody: Tell the traveller what to grab.
tripDetailMobile.belowMinBody: Minimum slot fee for this trip is €{min}.
tripDetailMobile.paymentHoldBody: This trip uses a card hold. Open the site to finish authorization.
tripDetailMobile.feeFootnote: Charged when the seller confirms your reservation. Card holds and Stripe checkout are on the web today when Connect is enabled for the route.

changelog.metaDescription: High-signal product releases for operators and investors.
changelog.title: Product log
changelog.subtitle: High-signal releases for operators and investors.

entries.title: Catalog photo gallery in product picker
entries.body: Pics: browse up to 60 catalog rows in a modal and tap to attach a product photo. Propose-product flow shows attached photos, clearer category/origin layout, and safer ISO2 input. Placeholder tiles when seed items have no image yet.

entries.title: Trip marketplace + sell-ready surface
entries.body: Trip offers and reservations, platform-fee logic, and operator tooling. Marketplace pages use live data; optional ENABLE_DEMO_MODE + labelled showcase for empty staging hosts. Curated catalog static fallback in API for unseeded dev. Operations docs under docs/ops/. Shared design tokens, marketing home, admin shell, mobile onboarding, OG image, expanded sitemap.

entries.title: Open beta scaffolding
entries.body: Next.js web, Expo mobile, Hono API, Drizzle schema, Meili search, PartyKit stub, i18n package.

productPicker.searchPlaceholder: Krówki Mleczne, Mastiha of Chios, Manner Original…
productPicker.emptyCatalogHint: W katalogu jeszcze nic nie ma. Dodaj produkt poniżej.
productPicker.pasteUrlFootnote: Pobieramy, usuwamy EXIF i hostujemy ponownie u Eushop.
productPicker.errorFileType: Wybierz obraz JPEG, PNG, WebP lub AVIF.
productPicker.proposeModalBody: Zweryfikujemy i dodamy do katalogu, żeby sąsiad od razu go znalazł.
productPicker.proposeDescriptionPlaceholder: Miękkie cukierki mleczne w czerwonym woskowym papierze, klasyk polskich wesel.

productPicker.proposePhotosStrip: Photos you attach (from search, upload, or Pics)

help.title: Help & frequently asked questions
help.subtitle: How matching, safety, fees, and your data work on Eushop.
help.contactCtaBody: For operations and trust & safety routing, use the contact page.

whatIsEushop.a: Eushop is a discovery and messaging layer for private individuals: spare luggage slots on trips people already take, open requests for specific items, and neighbourhood finder-fee listings. We are not a courier, customs broker, or warehouse.

matchingBasics.a: Trips, listings, and open requests are tied to places and corridors (countries, cities, geohash cells). Search and feeds surface what is near you or along a route; you agree details in chat. Automated matching signals may run in the background when jobs are configured.

openRequests.a: A buyer posts what they want within a radius and optional max finder fee. Sellers and travellers with overlapping routes or nearby listings can respond. Descriptions must follow our content rules; obvious restricted topics are blocked server-side.

fees.a: On confirmed trip reservations we take a small platform fee: min(€1.50, 12% of the agreed slot fee). Local listing finder fees are agreed between you and the other party; read trip and listing screens for the exact breakdown before you confirm.

payments.a: Many handoffs still settle off-platform or in person. Where Stripe Connect is enabled and keys are configured, card holds may apply for certain trip flows—if publishable keys are missing, that path is simply unavailable.

privacyGeo.q: Do you store my exact address?
privacyGeo.a: No. Listings and requests use a privacy cell (approximate area). Exact coordinates may exist server-side for distance math but are not exposed as a street address in the product.

demoMode.a: On some staging hosts, ENABLE_DEMO_MODE can show a labelled catalogue showcase when the live marketplace is empty. It never replaces traction metrics on /traction, which stay database-backed.

deleteAccount.q: How do I delete my account?
deleteAccount.a: From your profile you can export JSON (GDPR portability) and request permanent deletion. Cascading deletes remove listings, requests, and chats according to our retention policy.

reporting.q: How do I report abuse or scams?
reporting.a: Use in-app blocking where available and contact operations with links and screenshots. We prioritise safety issues; see the Safety pages for meet-up guidance.

analytics.q: How do analytics and cookies work?
analytics.a: Analytics only initialises after consent and when keys are configured. You may self-host PostHog or use EU PostHog Cloud; see docs/ops/zero-cost-stack.md for the full inventory.

inngestJobs.q: What happens if background jobs are off?
inngestJobs.a: Without Inngest keys, scheduled notifications and some indexing workflows do not run in that environment. Core browsing and CRUD still work against Postgres.

hrVolunteer.a: We are early-stage. The careers page lists how to reach us for collaborations; there may be no open payroll roles yet.

languages.a: The interface ships with many locale stubs (English strings until translated). Prefer reporting mistranslations via GitHub or email so we can prioritise real copy for your corridor.

legalNotAdvice.a: No. It summarises product behaviour. For regulated topics (food safety, customs, taxes) consult qualified professionals in your jurisdiction.

disputesBetweenUsers.q: What if the other person does not show up or the item is wrong?
disputesBetweenUsers.a: Try chat first, then use report flows. Card holds and refunds follow payment partner rules when checkout is enabled; peer-to-peer cash handoffs are outside our settlement control.

stripeConnectSeller.q: Who is the merchant of record on card payments?
stripeConnectSeller.a: When Stripe Connect is enabled, disclosures on the checkout screen name the party authorised to capture funds. Eushop provides software introductions; your contract for the goods remains with the other participant unless stated otherwise at payment time.

chargebackProcess.q: Can I charge back a trip fee?
chargebackProcess.a: You may ask your bank per network rules. Outcomes are decided by the issuer and scheme, not by Eushop. We cooperate with lawful information requests from processors.

vatAndTaxes.q: Does Eushop collect VAT on fees?
vatAndTaxes.a: Platform fee tax treatment depends on your country and our entity status—see checkout and receipt text when live. Cross-border food duties remain your responsibility.

kycVerifiedBringer.q: What does a verified bringer badge mean?
kycVerifiedBringer.a: It signals an identity check passed with our vendor at the time shown. It is not a food-safety endorsement, customs guarantee, or insurance policy.

minorsAllowed.a: You must meet the minimum age in your jurisdiction and be able to enter contracts. Parents or guardians handle accounts for younger household members where local law allows.

prohibitedItemsExamples.a: Weapons, drugs, stolen goods, regulated medicines without prescription flows, and other illegal content. Food copy is filtered for obvious restricted phrases; evasion can lead to suspension.

crossBorderCustoms.q: Who handles customs and import rules?
crossBorderCustoms.a: Travellers and buyers are responsible for declarations, duties, and admissibility. Eushop does not clear shipments or act as a customs broker.

listingVsRequest.q: What is the difference between a listing and an open request?
listingVsRequest.a: Listings advertise something you have nearby. Open requests state something you want within a radius. Both use approximate map cells, not exact street addresses.

tripCancellation.q: How do trip reservation cancellations work?
tripCancellation.a: Cancellation windows and refundability depend on the trip rules shown at booking and on processor capabilities. Read the trip detail screen before you authorise a hold.

accountSuspension.q: When can my account be suspended?
accountSuspension.a: For repeated policy violations, fraud signals, illegal content, or safety risks. We aim to be proportionate; serious cases may be immediate.

chatRetention.a: Retention follows our privacy policy and operational needs (moderation, disputes, legal holds). Export your data from profile where available.

photoGuidelines.a: Accurate images of the item or context, no explicit imagery, no photos of third parties without consent, no QR codes that bypass in-app safety.

insuranceNotProvided.a: No. Purchase travel or goods insurance independently if you need coverage for loss or spoilage.

lawEnforcementRequests.q: How does Eushop handle law enforcement requests?
lawEnforcementRequests.a: We review warrants and lawful process with counsel, narrow scope where possible, and notify users when law permits.

pressAndDMCA.q: I have a copyright complaint—where do I send it?
pressAndDMCA.a: Use the contact channel with full URLs, proof of ownership, and a good-faith statement. We process valid notices and counter-notices per applicable law.

accessibilityStatement.a: We aim for keyboard navigation and readable contrast on core flows. Report blockers via contact with pages and assistive tech used.

bugBounty.q: Do you run a bug bounty?
bugBounty.a: See SECURITY.md for coordinated disclosure. Do not test against production without written permission.

apiRateLimits.a: Yes. Automated scraping or credential stuffing may be blocked. Integrations should backoff and identify themselves responsibly.

thirdPartyCookies.q: Which third parties see my activity?
thirdPartyCookies.a: Only vendors listed in the privacy policy when enabled (e.g. analytics after consent, payment processors at checkout). No ads network sale of DMs.

subProcessors.a: Privacy policy and ops docs enumerate hosting, email, payments, and verification vendors as they are engaged.

dataPortabilityFormat.a: JSON bundles from profile export where implemented; attachments may be links with expiry.

parentalControls.a: Not as a dedicated product. Guardians should supervise minors’ device use and purchases.

brokerReselling.q: Can I run a buying service on Eushop?
brokerReselling.a: Commercial brokerage and storefront behaviour violate our private-use terms and may be removed.

alcoholTobaccoRules.q: Can I trade alcohol or tobacco?
alcoholTobaccoRules.a: Only where explicitly allowed by local law and product settings. Many corridors disallow it—check regulations before posting.

animalProductsRules.q: What about meat, dairy, or fish?
animalProductsRules.a: Highly regulated. Many borders restrict fresh animal products. You are responsible for temperature chain and import rules.

volunteerModeration.a: Internal staff and contracted reviewers may handle queues. Community reports are inputs, not votes that auto-remove content.

corridorDiscoveryBias.q: Why do I see certain countries first?
corridorDiscoveryBias.a: Discovery blends distance, freshness, and safety signals. It is not pay-to-rank for individuals; marketing placements, if any, will be labelled.

escalationToHuman.q: How do I reach a human?
escalationToHuman.a: Use /contact with operations email when configured. Safety-critical issues should include URLs and timestamps for faster routing.

dsaNotice.q: Where is the illegal content notice page?
dsaNotice.a: See /safety/report for the EU Digital Services Act-style notice and process summary. It complements in-app reporting buttons.

careers.title: Build with us
careers.subtitle: Small team, high craft, EU-first marketplace primitives.
careers.body1: Eushop connects diaspora corridors with honest tooling: trips, open asks, and local shares. We optimise for trust, privacy-preserving geo, and clear fees—not growth hacks.
careers.body2: We value shipping, writing, and operational rigour. If you believe in open layers for peer exchange, send a short note with what you would improve first.
careers.openRolesBody: Roles are posted here as we open them. Until then, speculative applications are welcome via the contact channel configured for your deployment.

contact.title: Contact & escalation
contact.subtitle: Reach the operations inbox configured for this deployment.
contact.responseTime: We read every message; response time depends on team capacity.
contact.notConfigured: Not configured for this deployment. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_OPERATIONS_EMAIL (and optionally NEXT_PUBLIC_OPERATIONS_PHONE_E164) in your environment.
contact.abuseBody: For harassment, fraud, or illegal content reports, include URLs, user handles, and timestamps. See /safety for meet-up guidance.
contact.pressNote: Press and media kits live on /press.

requestsPage.explainerTitle: Open requests connect demand to supply.
requestsPage.explainerBody: Post what you need within a radius and optional max finder fee. Travellers with spare capacity and neighbours with listings see your ask in discovery. Exact addresses stay private; we use approximate cells.
requestsPage.explainerFees: You agree the handoff and payment method in chat unless in-app card capture is enabled for your environment.
requestsPage.explainerModeration: Descriptions are checked against basic safety rules. Repeat abuse can lead to suspension.
requestsPage.metaDescription: See what neighbours are asking for. Match on your next trip or listing.

requestsNew.explainerHint: Be specific about product, quantity, and freshness. Avoid restricted or illegal items—see Help & FAQ.

illegalContentReport.metaDescription: How to notify Eushop about illegal content, safety risks, and serious policy breaches.
illegalContentReport.title: Illegal content & serious abuse
illegalContentReport.subtitle: Transparency summary for notices (including EU Digital Services Act-style reporting).

whatToReport.title: What belongs here
whatToReport.body: Use this page when you need a durable public reference for serious issues that may not fit a single in-app button.
whatToReport.b1: Illegal goods or services, credible threats, child safety concerns, non-consensual imagery, terrorism glorification, or malware distribution.
whatToReport.b2: Large-scale fraud rings, impersonation of Eushop staff, or attempts to harvest credentials at scale.
whatToReport.b3: Copyright or trademark disputes where you can supply ownership evidence.
whatToReport.b4: EU illegal content notices: include your name, contact, item URL(s), unlawful nature, and good-faith statement.

howToReport.title: How to send a notice
howToReport.body: Email the operations address configured for this deployment (see /contact). Subject lines like “Illegal content notice” or “DSA notice” help routing.
howToReport.include: Attach permalinks, UTC timestamps, screenshots, and any reference IDs from in-app reports. Multiple URLs in one mail are fine.

process.title: What we do next
process.body: We acknowledge receipt when possible, triage by severity, and may restrict visibility of content pending review. Timelines depend on volume and legal process; emergencies (imminent harm) are prioritised. Outcomes may include removal, account suspension, or referral to authorities when required.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.body: Aggregate enforcement statistics may be published when counsel agrees. Individual decisions are communicated to the notifier when policy allows.

notLegal.title: Not legal advice
notLegal.body: This page summarises intent and routing. Final procedures follow counsel-approved policies and applicable law.

manifestoPage.metaDescription: The last-mile is already going somewhere. The Eushop manifesto for a polite, peer-powered diaspora marketplace.
manifestoPage.title: The last-mile is already going somewhere.
manifestoPage.subtitle: Every weekend someone is already crossing a border with room in the bag. The gap is coordination—not another freight company.
manifestoPage.intro1: Every weekend a Polish nurse in Munich wires twenty euros to her cousin in Warsaw for a tube of Wedel chocolate, a packet of Krówki, a jar of Łowicz beetroot. The cousin walks three hundred metres to a corner shop, pays four euros, hands the bag to a friend who is already booked on Saturday's LO 369 to Munich, and the next morning the chocolate is on a kitchen table in Schwabing.
manifestoPage.intro2: The transaction works. The transaction has always worked. What it lacks is a coordination layer that two strangers from the same country, who haven't met yet, can both trust.
manifestoPage.c1Title: 1. The marginal cost of a spare bag slot is zero.
manifestoPage.c1Body: Diaspora travel is already booked, already paid for, already at the airport. Three empty kilos in a bag are a wasted resource the way an empty Uber seat was a wasted resource in 2008. We are the matchmaker, not the carrier.
manifestoPage.c2Body: We will not import. We will not warehouse. We will not pretend to be a courier company. The handoff stays human, in a café, in a public square, between two people who chose each other. We charge a small platform fee on the reservation — that is all.
manifestoPage.c3Title: 3. Trust is the moat. Privacy is the floor.
manifestoPage.c3Body: Every map we render is a five-kilometre cell with a deterministic jitter. Every chat is end-of-conversation expirable. Every piece of personal data lives in a Hetzner rack in Falkenstein. We can prove this in code, line by line.
manifestoPage.willNot1: We will not sell ads against your search for "Aromat".
manifestoPage.willNot2: We will not let commercial importers post under a personal profile.
manifestoPage.willNot3: We will not charge buyers for the privilege of finding their own neighbours.
manifestoPage.willNot4: We will not pretend to handle customs declarations, allergen labelling, or cold-chain logistics. Those are not our promises to keep.
manifestoPage.willNot5: We will not export this trust graph to anyone, ever.
manifestoPage.whyNowBody: Post-Brexit re-shoring. Inflation that turned a €4 import into a €19 import. Open Food Facts maturing into a serious commons. The first cohort of European founders comfortable building a marketplace that is small, polite, rooted in European trust norms—and built to work anywhere people already travel.
manifestoPage.whyNowClosing: The neighbour with the suitcase is already there. We are just turning on the light.
manifestoPage.signoff: — The Eushop founding team. Munich, Warsaw, Lisbon, Athens, Tallinn.

pitchPage.metaDescription: Investor narrative: problem, wedge, GTM, and ask — without mixing historic statistics and forward-looking TAM.
pitchPage.title: Eushop in one sitting.
pitchPage.subtitle: Problem, wedge, GTM, and what we need next—without mixing historic statistics and forward-looking TAM.
pitchPage.problemBody: People are always on the move, and they bring things back for each other — but that trade is fragmented across group chats, DMs, and luck. There is no trusted layer for "who near me, or on my route, has this niche thing right now?" The category is global; we are starting where it is densest.
pitchPage.solutionBody: Eushop is discovery + messaging with privacy-preserving cells: trip reservations use an agreed slot fee; local shares and requests keep finder's-fee wording where it matches the schema. Handoffs stay in public. Web, admin, and Expo share one design language and one API surface (tRPC + Postgres + Meili).
pitchPage.gtmBody: Wedge: dense EU corridors with community partners, then expand along rail and air hubs. Destination: a global peer layer for trips, locals, and open asks. Catalog is seeded; growth is user-generated listings, requests, and trip offers.
pitchPage.askBody: Capital to harden trust & safety, ship native payments rails where legal, and hire a small moderation pod per time zone. We're building the polite corner of peer trade for things people already carry — starting in Europe, designed to travel.
pitchPage.mobilityBefore: Large-scale cross-border residence inside Europe is a long-documented statistical fact. We cite official publications from 2007 and earlier (plus Eurostat's first 2007 headline release, documented in 2008) when we need that framing—see
pitchPage.mobilityAfter: . We do not treat a headline "millions" number as revenue TAM without a defined cohort and a matching source.

roadmapPage.metaDescription: Indicative product horizons for trips, trust, payments, and native polish — ship order may shift.
roadmapPage.title: Where we are headed.
roadmapPage.subtitle: Indicative horizons — ship order may shift with regulation and community feedback.
roadmapPage.phase1Body: First-class flows to publish real routes, dates, spare volume/weight, and legs. Match travellers to catalog items and freeform requests—still location-first.
roadmapPage.phase2Body: Moderation tooling, repeat-handoff prompts, corridor playbooks, Meilisearch tuning, and optional restock alerts for saved corridors.
roadmapPage.phase3Body: Optional in-app settlement for finder fees on local shares and for trip slot fees; fixed price first, then pilot auctions where regulation allows.
roadmapPage.phase4Body: Photo-first profiles and reputation signals; offline maps for cells, richer item graph, retailer opt-in exports.
